q: A resume call returns a fresh session instead of the prior history. What is the most common cause?
opts: The cwd at resume time differs from the cwd where the session was created
The Anthropic API rotated the session ID
explain: Sessions key off cwd. If you resume from a different working directory, the SDK can't find the encoded-cwd folder and starts a new session. Either run from the same cwd or use a SessionStore adapter for cross-host resume.

q: You extract a 'PolicyNumber' field from 1000 documents. How should you validate confidence calibration?
opts: A single accuracy number across the full corpus is sufficient
Stratified random sampling across document types AND fields
Sample only the documents Claude flagged as low-confidence
Average string lengths as a proxy
explain: Confidence calibration must be validated by document type AND field via stratified random sampling. A single global accuracy hides per-segment failures.

q: For provenance tracking on a research synthesis, which structure is recommended?
opts: claim/evidence/source/confidence with publication dates, preserved through synthesis
Inline footnote markers without structured metadata
explain: Structured provenance pairs each claim with evidence, source, confidence, and dates. Synthesis steps must preserve attribution rather than collapsing it into anonymous prose.

q: Which beta header is required to use the Files API?
opts: No header; Files API is GA
explain: Files API is in beta and requires the header 'anthropic-beta: files-api-2025-04-14'. It supports upload, download (only for files generated by code execution or skills), list, retrieve metadata, and delete.

q: What is the per-file size limit on the Files API?
explain: Files API supports up to 500 MB per file with a total of 500 GB per organization.

q: What is the per-organization storage limit on the Files API?
explain: Per-organization storage cap is 500 GB on the Files API.

q: In Claude Code, where does auto memory store its files for a given repository?
explain: Claude Code's auto memory writes to ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/. The <project> path is derived from the git repository so all worktrees share one memory dir.

q: How much of MEMORY.md does Claude Code load at session start?
opts: The entire file regardless of size
First 200 lines or 25KB, whichever comes first
explain: Claude Code loads the first 200 lines or 25KB of MEMORY.md, whichever is smaller. Topic files in the same dir are read on demand, not at startup.

q: What are the three core context engineering pillars from Anthropic's effective context engineering guidance?
opts: Just-in-time retrieval, structured note-taking, sub-agent isolation
explain: The three pillars are: just-in-time retrieval (load by reference, not eager), structured note-taking (scratchpads/memory), and sub-agent architecture (isolated contexts with summaries returned).

q: In the orchestrator-worker pattern, what is the recommended way to keep detailed search context out of the orchestrator?
opts: Sub-agent isolation: detailed context stays in subagents; only condensed summaries flow up
Run subagents in the same context window as the orchestrator
explain: Sub-agent isolation keeps detailed exploration in each subagent's own context window and returns only condensed summaries to the orchestrator, achieving separation of concerns.

q: Per Anthropic's multi-agent research write-up, what is the approximate token multiplier of a multi-agent system vs a chat interaction?
explain: Multi-agent systems use roughly 15x more tokens than chat interactions; agents alone use ~4x. The trade-off is justified only when task value exceeds the cost premium.

q: What is the recommended state-passing pattern between subagents and the orchestrator to minimize context overhead?
opts: Pass full tool outputs through assistant messages
Subagents store work in external systems and pass lightweight references back
Use the system prompt as a dropbox
explain: Subagents call tools to store outputs in shared filesystem state, then pass back lightweight references. This reduces token overhead and avoids information loss in the orchestrator.

q: What XML element delivers the initial token budget to context-aware models?
explain: Context-aware models receive <budget:token_budget>1000000</budget:token_budget> (or 200000) at session start, then <system_warning> updates after tool calls.

q: Extended thinking blocks from prior turns: which statement is true?
opts: You must always pass them back manually
The Claude API automatically strips thinking blocks from prior turns when calculating context
They are billed as input tokens on every turn
They count toward the user's max_tokens budget
explain: Prior thinking blocks are automatically excluded from context window calculation. Effective: context_window = (input_tokens - previous_thinking_tokens) + current_turn_tokens. They are billed as output once, when generated.

q: During tool use with extended thinking, when MUST you return the original thinking block back to the API?
opts: When posting tool_result that follows a tool_use that came with that thinking block; the cryptographic signature is verified
explain: The unmodified thinking block (with signature) MUST be passed back alongside its corresponding tool_result. Modifications break signature verification and the API errors.

q: You added a text block immediately after a tool_result and now Claude returns near-empty 'end_turn' responses. Why?
opts: Claude learns to expect user input after every tool result and ends its turn to follow that pattern
explain: Adding text blocks immediately after a tool_result trains the model to expect a user-provided text follow-up, so it ends its turn. Send only the tool_result content; do not append text in the same user turn.

q: Which strategy clears 'thinking' blocks while letting you preserve recent reasoning?
explain: clear_thinking_20251015 manages thinking block retention with a 'keep' parameter. Default behavior depends on model class: newer Opus/Sonnet keep all; Haiku and older models keep only the last turn.

q: Which parameter on clear_tool_uses guarantees a minimum token reduction per clearing event?
explain: clear_at_least sets a minimum number of tokens that must be removed when clearing fires. This is critical for offsetting cache rewrite costs.

q: Which clear_tool_uses parameter prevents specific tools from being cleared?
explain: exclude_tools accepts a list of tool names that must not be cleared (e.g., ['web_search'] to keep search results).

q: Within an Agent SDK session, which mechanism revertable file changes the agent made?
explain: Sessions persist conversation, not filesystem state. To snapshot and revert agent file edits, use file checkpointing, a separate Agent SDK feature.

q: You want a stateless Agent SDK call that writes nothing to disk (TypeScript). What option achieves this?
explain: Setting persistSession: false in the TypeScript SDK keeps the session in memory only for the duration of the call. Python always persists to disk.

q: What is the correct precedence order across CLAUDE.md scopes in Claude Code?
opts: Local > Project > User > Managed Policy (more specific takes precedence)
explain: CLAUDE.md files are concatenated from filesystem root down. Within each level, CLAUDE.local.md is appended after CLAUDE.md. More specific (closer to cwd) effectively reads last.

q: What is the recommended target size limit for a CLAUDE.md file?
explain: Target under 200 lines per CLAUDE.md. Longer files consume more context and reduce adherence. Use path-scoped rules in .claude/rules/ for instructions only relevant to specific files.

q: You want an instruction to load only when Claude opens TypeScript files in src/api/. Where should you put it?
opts: A markdown file in .claude/rules/ with paths frontmatter matching the glob
explain: .claude/rules/*.md files with YAML 'paths' frontmatter (e.g., paths: ['src/api/**/*.ts']) load only when Claude reads matching files, saving context space.

q: After /compact in Claude Code, which content is automatically re-injected into the session from disk?
explain: Project-root CLAUDE.md survives compaction: Claude re-reads it from disk and re-injects it. Nested CLAUDE.md in subdirs reload on demand when their files are next read.

q: Which of the following is the WRONG reason to escalate to a human in a support agent?
opts: Policy is silent on this case
No-progress detection: agent has tried multiple paths and is stuck
explain: Self-reported confidence is unreliable for routing. The valid triggers are: explicit human request, policy gap, or inability to progress. Confidence should not gate escalation.

q: Two web sources disagree on a number. The agent silently averages them and reports a single value. Why is this wrong?
opts: It destroys provenance and hides the disagreement from downstream users
It violates the Files API rate limit
explain: Silent averaging or picking destroys provenance. Always annotate both values with attribution and dates so the consumer can resolve the conflict explicitly.

q: A tool returns []. The error field is null. The agent says 'I could not access the system.' What's the bug?
opts: The agent should always escalate empty results
The agent failed to distinguish a valid empty result from an access failure
The tool is broken; no client-side bug
explain: With error=null and empty rows, this is a valid empty result, not an access failure. Conflating the two fabricates outage stories. Inspect status fields explicitly.

q: You enable the memory tool but the model never reads memory before answering. What system instruction is automatically prepended?
opts: 'Memory is optional; use only if asked.'
'IMPORTANT: ALWAYS VIEW YOUR MEMORY DIRECTORY BEFORE DOING ANYTHING ELSE.'
'Skip memory unless the user mentions it.'
explain: When the memory tool is enabled, an instruction is automatically inserted: 'IMPORTANT: ALWAYS VIEW YOUR MEMORY DIRECTORY BEFORE DOING ANYTHING ELSE.' followed by a memory protocol prompt.

q: Compaction and the memory tool together: what is each responsible for?
opts: Compaction = server-side summarization of conversation; memory tool = client-side persistence across sessions
Both run server-side and store identical state
Both run client-side and are interchangeable
explain: Compaction is server-side summarization that compresses old conversation. The memory tool runs client-side and persists structured info across sessions. Use them together: compaction handles conversation; memory persists durable knowledge.

q: Server-side compaction handles compression where? Memory tool persists where?
opts: Compaction at Anthropic; memory in your client storage
Compaction in client; memory at Anthropic
explain: Compaction is server-side: Anthropic generates the summary block. Memory tool is client-side: your application stores files. The two are complementary, not redundant.

q: You run a long research session and want both compaction and memory. Why use both?
opts: They are mutually exclusive; pick one
Compaction keeps active conversation manageable; memory ensures critical info survives compaction summary loss
Compaction can only summarize what memory has not stored
explain: Compaction may summarize away details. Memory persists durable info (decisions, key facts) across compaction boundaries so nothing important is lost in the summary.

q: What is the correct cache_control type as currently supported?
explain: Only 'ephemeral' is supported as a cache_control type today. The TTL field selects 5m or 1h.

q: How does the docs recommend pre-warming a cache without paying for output tokens?
opts: Set max_tokens=0 in a request that includes the cache_control block; cache write is paid but no output is billed
explain: Pre-warm by sending the request with max_tokens=0. The cache write is incurred but no output tokens are billed. Subsequent real requests get instant cache hits.

q: What is the lookback window for cache_control breakpoint matching?
explain: The system checks up to 20 positions backward per breakpoint, finding entries prior requests already wrote. Cache writes happen only at marked breakpoints.

q: After /compact in Claude Code, which of these is most likely to be lost?
opts: An instruction the user typed only into chat (never written to CLAUDE.md or auto memory)
explain: Conversation-only instructions disappear after compaction. Project-root CLAUDE.md is re-read from disk; auto memory persists; chat-only directives don't. Add lasting rules to CLAUDE.md.

q: Which Claude Code memory storage is machine-local and not synced across hosts?
explain: Auto memory is machine-local: all worktrees of a repo on a machine share one auto memory dir, but it is not shared across machines or cloud envs. Use CLAUDE.md (committed) for cross-machine instructions.

q: Within usage object, which two fields confirm caching engaged?
explain: cache_creation_input_tokens shows tokens just cached; cache_read_input_tokens shows tokens read from cache. Both 0 means caching did not engage.

q: How many images or PDF pages can a single 1M-context request include?
explain: 1M-context-capable models accept up to 600 images or PDF pages per request (vs 100 for 200k-context models). Watch the request size limit.

q: Which models silently truncate input vs return a validation error when context overflows?
opts: Newer models (Sonnet 3.7+ generation onward) return validation error rather than silently truncating
explain: Newer Claude models (starting Sonnet 3.7 generation) return validation errors when input + output exceed context, instead of silent truncation. Use the token counting API to plan.

q: You want to reuse a transcript on a fresh CI worker (different host) for resume. What is required?
opts: Persist ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl and restore it on the new host at the same path with matching cwd
explain: Sessions are local to the machine that created them. To resume cross-host, copy the JSONL to the same path under matching cwd, or use a SessionStore adapter for shared storage.

q: For multi-session projects, what is the safest pattern for an agent picking up where it left off?
opts: Re-run all earlier tasks each session
On session start, read MEMORY.md (auto memory) and structured CLAUDE.md, then resume work using their state
Only use server-side compaction; ignore local memory
Disable memory and rely on max_tokens
explain: The documented multi-session software development pattern uses memory artifacts (progress log, feature checklist, references) plus CLAUDE.md so each new session reconstructs state in seconds without re-exploring the codebase.

q: Within a context-aware Sonnet 4.6 session, what do <system_warning> messages communicate?
opts: Token usage X/Y after each tool call so the model can pace remaining work
explain: Context-aware models (Sonnet 4.6/4.5, Haiku 4.5) receive <system_warning>Token usage: X/Y; remaining: Z</system_warning> after each tool call to budget remaining work.

q: Which of the following is NOT a documented context engineering pillar?
explain: The three documented pillars are just-in-time retrieval, structured note-taking, and sub-agent isolation. Sentiment-driven escalation is explicitly discouraged for routing.

q: You want path-scoped instructions across multiple repos. Which file convention applies?
opts: CLAUDE.md must contain all path rules inline
explain: Place markdown files in .claude/rules/ with YAML 'paths' frontmatter. Files without 'paths' load unconditionally; with paths, they trigger when Claude reads matching files.

q: Why is forkSession safer than running two parallel resume calls on the same session_id?
opts: resume can corrupt the JSONL on concurrent writes; fork creates a new ID
resume requires authentication; fork does not
explain: Concurrent writes to the same session JSONL are unsafe. Forking creates a distinct session ID with its own transcript so two paths can run independently.

q: Auto memory in Claude Code will save a note when:
opts: Claude decides the information would be useful in a future conversation
explain: Auto memory does not save every session. Claude decides what's worth remembering based on whether it would help a future conversation. Files are plain markdown editable via /memory.

q: How do you disable Claude Code auto memory via environment variable?
explain: Set CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1, or toggle via /memory in session, or set autoMemoryEnabled: false in settings.

q: In an agentic workflow, which combination is the recommended starting point for context management?
opts: Server-side compaction (with compact-2026-01-12) plus memory tool for durable info
Build a custom summarizer in your client
explain: Anthropic recommends server-side compaction as the primary strategy for long-running conversations, paired with the memory tool for durable knowledge. Context editing is for finer-grained needs.

q: You see cache_creation_input_tokens=0 and cache_read_input_tokens=0 with cache_control set. Most likely cause?
opts: The block is below the model's minimum cacheable tokens (e.g., 4096 for Opus 4.7)
explain: Below-minimum requests silently no-op without error. Verify minimums (Opus 4.7: 4096; Sonnet 4.6: 2048; Haiku 4.5: 4096) and inspect both usage fields.

q: Which of the following is NOT a memory tool command?
explain: The 6 commands are view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. There is no execute command; the memory tool only manipulates files.

q: You enable the memory tool but the model frequently overwrites stale entries instead of organizing the directory. What guidance helps?
opts: Add an instruction to keep memory up-to-date, coherent, and organized; rename or delete files no longer relevant
Force Claude to use 1h cache TTL
explain: The docs offer a recommended addendum prompt directing Claude to keep memory tidy: rename or delete obsolete files; do not create new files unnecessarily.

q: Which of these is the correct beta header to enable model_context_window_exceeded on older models that don't return it by default?
opts: No header is needed; all models return it
explain: For older models, the beta header 'model-context-window-exceeded-2025-08-26' enables this stop reason. Sonnet 4.5 and newer return it by default.

q: In long agent sessions, the recommended scratchpad pattern uses which substrate?
opts: Persistent files (filesystem or memory tool) referenced by lightweight identifiers
explain: Just-in-time retrieval keeps lightweight identifiers (paths, IDs, links) in context and loads details on demand. Scratchpad files in memory or filesystem persist findings across context boundaries.

q: Which content cannot be cached even when wrapped in a cache_control block?
opts: Empty text blocks and citations sources
explain: Empty text blocks and citations cannot be cached. Cache the underlying source documents instead. Tools, system messages, text, images, documents, tool_use, and tool_result are cacheable.

q: You use 1h TTL caching for your system prompt. What is the trade-off vs 5m?
opts: 1h writes cost 2x base vs 5m writes 1.25x base; reads are 0.1x for both
explain: 1h cache writes are 2x base; 5m writes are 1.25x base. Reads are 0.1x for both. Choose 1h when reuse interval exceeds 5 minutes and the higher write premium is justified.

q: In the multi-agent research write-up, what was the documented performance lift over single-agent on internal evaluations?
explain: Anthropic's multi-agent research system showed 90.2% better performance on internal breadth-first eval suites versus single-agent baselines, justifying the 15x token premium for high-value tasks.

q: Which of these is NOT a stop_reason value documented in the API?
explain: Documented stop_reason values: end_turn, tool_use, max_tokens, stop_sequence, refusal, pause_turn, model_context_window_exceeded. There is no cache_invalidation stop reason.

q: In agent loops using server tools (web_search/web_fetch), what should you implement?
opts: A pause_turn handler that resends the assistant content unchanged to continue
explain: With server tools, the server-side loop may hit its iteration cap and return pause_turn. Add a handler that resends the current assistant content as-is for continuation, with a max_continuations cap.

q: You want to clear stale tool results but preserve the most recent N. Which parameter controls 'how much to keep'?
explain: The 'keep' object on clear_tool_uses_20250919 (e.g., {type: 'tool_uses', value: 3}) preserves the most recent N tool uses. 'trigger' decides when clearing fires; 'clear_at_least' enforces a minimum reduction; 'exclude_tools' protects specific tool names.

q: Which of these is the strongest justification for adopting a multi-agent (orchestrator-worker) architecture?
opts: Breadth-first queries with parallel independent investigations and isolated context per subagent
explain: Multi-agent architectures excel at breadth-first queries that pursue multiple independent directions in parallel and benefit from isolated context per subagent. They cost ~15x more tokens, justified by task value.

q: After /init in Claude Code, what does it do if a CLAUDE.md already exists?
opts: Creates CLAUDE.local.md instead and ignores the original
explain: If CLAUDE.md already exists, /init suggests improvements rather than overwriting. CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT=1 enables a multi-phase interactive flow that proposes changes for review.

q: Why is a 'persistent case facts' block placed at the top of context (not the bottom)?
opts: Top placement counters lost-in-the-middle and ensures the model sees critical facts before reasoning over recent turns
Caching only works on top blocks
explain: Top-of-context placement is recommended because lost-in-the-middle degrades recall for content placed deep in long contexts. Pinning case facts at the top maximizes the chance the model uses them.

q: Which approach correctly handles error_max_turns / error_max_budget_usd in the Agent SDK?
opts: Capture session_id from the result, then resume with a higher limit to continue from where it stopped
explain: On error_max_turns or error_max_budget_usd, the result still includes session_id. Resume the same session with raised limits so the agent picks up with full prior context.

q: Which file in Claude Code is read but cannot be excluded by individual settings?
explain: Managed policy CLAUDE.md (e.g., /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/CLAUDE.md on macOS) cannot be excluded via claudeMdExcludes; it ensures org-wide instructions always apply.

q: You see frequent stop_reason: refusal on Sonnet 4.5. What does the docs suggest as a possible alternative?
opts: Try Haiku 4.5 (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) which has different usage restrictions
Pad the prompt with an authority claim
explain: The docs note: if refusals are frequent on Sonnet 4.5 / Opus 4.1, try Haiku 4.5 which has different API safety filters. Never attempt to disable safety filters via headers.

q: For a 200k-context model receiving an empty assistant 'end_turn' response right after tool results, the recommended next step is:
opts: Send the empty response back as input
Add a NEW user message with a continuation prompt (e.g., 'Please continue')
explain: You cannot retry by re-sending the empty response: Claude already decided it's done. Add a new user message asking it to continue. Better: ensure you don't add text blocks immediately after tool_result.

q: Which part of the cache hierarchy is invalidated when you toggle web_search on or off?
opts: tools cache is preserved; system and messages are invalidated
explain: Per the docs, toggling web_search/citations preserves the tools cache but invalidates system and messages. Tool definition changes invalidate everything.

q: Which change invalidates all three cache levels (tools, system, messages)?
explain: Changing tool definitions invalidates tools, system, and messages all together. Image addition and thinking parameter changes invalidate system and messages but preserve tools.

q: Which of the following correctly summarizes the 'just-in-time retrieval' principle?
opts: Eagerly load all candidate documents at session start
Maintain lightweight identifiers (file paths, queries, links) and dynamically load data into context only when needed
Pre-compute all answers and cache them
Run a separate retrieval Claude call before every user turn
explain: Just-in-time retrieval keeps lightweight references in context and pulls actual data only when needed via tool calls, keeping the active window focused on currently relevant content.

q: A teammate suggests caching every system prompt with TTL=1h regardless of size. What's the issue?
opts: If the prompt is below the minimum (e.g., <2048 tokens for Sonnet 4.6), it silently no-ops and you pay no cache benefit
explain: Caching silently no-ops below the model's minimum (Opus 4.7: 4096; Sonnet 4.6: 2048; Haiku 4.5: 4096). Verify with cache_creation_input_tokens / cache_read_input_tokens. 1h adds 2x write premium that's wasted if no cache writes.

q: You need to escalate when an agent stalls. Which structured signal helps detect 'inability to progress'?
opts: Repeated identical tool calls or no new information after N attempts
explain: Inability to progress is detected via deterministic signals like repeated identical tool calls or N attempts without new information. Self-confidence and sentiment are unreliable.

q: Memory tool 'view' on a directory returns:
opts: A listing up to 2 levels deep with sizes, excluding hidden items and node_modules
A JSON object of every file recursively
explain: The 'view' command on a directory returns files and dirs up to 2 levels deep with human-readable sizes (e.g., 1.5K), excluding hidden items and node_modules, separated by tab between size and path.

q: Why is preserving thinking blocks in cache (vs clearing) sometimes preferred?
opts: Thinking blocks are billed as input on every reuse
When kept, the prompt cache is preserved for cache hits; when cleared, the cache is invalidated at the clear point
Thinking blocks are required for streaming
explain: Keeping thinking blocks preserves cache hits and reduces input token costs. Clearing invalidates the cache at the clear point. Choose 'keep' based on whether you prioritize cache reuse or context space.

q: For a project that loads CLAUDE.md from an additional directory via --add-dir, what enables auto-loading of CLAUDE.md from that dir?
explain: By default, --add-dir does not load CLAUDE.md from additional directories. Set CLAUDE_CODE_ADDITIONAL_DIRECTORIES_CLAUDE_MD=1 to load CLAUDE.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/*.md, and CLAUDE.local.md from those dirs.

q: What is the recommended pattern for sub-agents needing their own persistent learnings across runs?
opts: Subagents maintain their own auto memory via subagent configuration
Subagents must persist via the orchestrator's session
explain: Subagents in Claude Code can maintain their own auto memory; configure via subagent options (enable persistent memory). This isolates learnings per role and avoids cross-pollution.

q: You want to enforce a hook that runs before every commit, not rely on CLAUDE.md to remind Claude. Why?
opts: CLAUDE.md is a hard enforcement layer
CLAUDE.md is context (behavioral), not enforced configuration; hooks execute as shell commands at fixed lifecycle events
explain: CLAUDE.md instructions shape behavior but aren't enforced. For things that MUST run at lifecycle events (pre-commit, post-edit), use hooks: the harness runs them as shell commands regardless of model decisions.

q: Which is FALSE about server-side compaction?
opts: It supports Opus 4.7, Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, and Mythos
It is supported on Haiku 4.5 with no header
Trigger threshold must be at least 50,000 input tokens
explain: Compaction is NOT supported on Haiku 4.5. Supported models: Mythos Preview, Opus 4.7, Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6. Beta header compact-2026-01-12 is required.

q: In an agent loop, which design avoids re-sending the entire conversation history each turn?
opts: Agent SDK sessions (resume/continue) which append-only persist the JSONL transcript
explain: The Agent SDK persists sessions as JSONL on disk and resumes/continues append-only. You don't manually concatenate; the SDK handles the transcript and re-injects context.

q: Which of these patterns is documented as the safest for cross-host session resume?
opts: Move JSONL files between hosts; ensure cwd matches
explain: To resume across hosts, move ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl to the same path on the new host with matching cwd. Or use a SessionStore adapter for shared storage.

q: You enable the Files API. What is true about file lifecycle?
opts: Files persist until you delete them; deletion is irreversible
Files auto-delete after every Messages call
Files require a daily heartbeat call to remain available
explain: Files persist indefinitely until explicitly deleted. Deletion is irreversible (per data retention policy). File API operations (upload/download/list/delete) are free; content used in Messages is billed as input tokens.

q: Your agentic loop currently checks if the assistant's response contains the phrase 'I've completed the task' to decide when to stop iterating. During testing, the agent sometimes generates this phrase mid-conversation while still needing to call additional tools. What should you change?
opts: Check stop_reason: continue when 'tool_use', terminate when 'end_turn'
Parse the response for tool call JSON
explain: stop_reason is the API-provided mechanism for determining whether the model wants to call a tool or has finished.

q: Your agentic loop processes tool results but does not append them to conversation history before sending the next request. The agent appears to forget tool outputs and re-calls the same tools. Root cause?
opts: Tool results must be appended to conversation history so the model can reason about them
explain: Tool results must be added to conversation history between iterations. Without this, the model has no record of previous outputs.

q: Your agentic loop has max_iterations=5 as primary stopping condition. The agent frequently hits this limit mid-task. Better approach?
opts: Use stop_reason as primary, iteration cap as safety fallback
explain: Primary loop control should be stop_reason. Iteration cap is only a safety fallback.

q: After a response with stop_reason 'tool_use', what should happen next?
opts: Send only the tool result as user message
Append assistant's response (with tool call) AND tool result, then send full updated history
Parse the tool result and summarize in next system prompt
Store result in DB and send reference ID
explain: Complete history including the tool call request and result must be appended and sent.

q: You check 'if response.content[0].type == "text": break'. Why is this incorrect?
opts: Content array can contain BOTH text and tool_use blocks in same response
Tool calls never mixed with text
explain: A single assistant response can contain both text blocks and tool_use blocks. Checking text as completion is anti-pattern.

q: Production agent enters infinite loop calling same two tools alternately. Best mitigation?
opts: Reasonable iteration cap as fallback + monitor for repeated identical calls
Ask 'Are you done?' after each call
Limit each tool to once per session
explain: Iteration cap prevents infinite loops; monitoring identifies root causes (poor tool descriptions, ambiguous results).

q: Distinguishes model-driven decision-making in agentic loop from pre-configured decision tree?
opts: In model-driven, Claude reasons about which tool to call next based on context, vs fixed sequence
Decision trees use API; model-driven uses local
explain: Model-driven analyzes context dynamically; decision trees follow hardcoded logic.

q: stop_reason 'max_tokens' indicates and how to handle?
opts: Response was truncated; continue conversation or increase max_tokens
Wants to call tool but ran out; terminate
explain: max_tokens means response was cut off. Distinct from end_turn (chose to stop) and tool_use (wants tool).

q: Synthesis agent misses findings from document analysis. Coordinator passes only summary reference. Most likely fix?
opts: Include complete findings from document analysis directly in synthesis subagent's prompt
Have synthesis call document analysis directly
explain: Subagent context must be explicitly provided. No automatic inheritance.

q: Coordinator needs web search, document analysis (independent), and fact checking (depends on both). Optimal pattern?
opts: Coordinator emits two Task tool calls in single response (web + doc parallel), then fact checking next turn
Run all parallel, fact checking polls
explain: Parallel subagents via multiple Task calls in single response. Web and doc are independent.

q: Coordinator forgets 'Task' in allowedTools. What happens?
explain: allowedTools must explicitly include 'Task' for coordinator to invoke subagents.

q: Three subagents researching 'renewable energy policy' produce overlapping/duplicate findings. Most effective fix?
opts: Partition research scope across subagents to minimize overlap
Each checks with coordinator before each subtopic
explain: Coordinator partitions scope: distinct subtopics or source types per agent.

q: Subagent encounters error analyzing document and sends message DIRECTLY to synthesis subagent. Why problematic?
opts: Subagents cannot communicate; all goes through coordinator for observability
Synthesis might not be running yet
Direct fine but should use different format
explain: Hub-and-spoke: coordinator manages all inter-subagent communication.

q: Coordinator routes EVERY query through full pipeline even for simple factual questions. What to change?
opts: Coordinator analyzes query requirements and dynamically selects subagents
explain: Coordinator should dynamically assess complexity and invoke only needed subagents.

q: Colleague suggests step-by-step procedural coordinator instructions: 'Step 1: Call web search with X. Step 2: Take result and call doc analysis'. Suboptimal because?
opts: Coordinator should specify research goals and quality criteria, not procedures, for adaptability
explain: Goal-oriented prompts let coordinator adapt strategy by query type.

q: Explore two testing strategies from a shared analysis baseline. Initial analysis already done. Best approach?
opts: Two new sessions, redo analysis each
fork_session creates independent branches from shared baseline
Same session sequentially with context clears
Two subagents each receive analysis copy
explain: fork_session creates independent branches, retaining initial context.

q: Agent skips get_customer and calls process_refund directly 8% of cases despite system prompt. Strongest guarantee?
opts: Programmatic prerequisite blocking process_refund until get_customer returns verified ID
explain: Programmatic prerequisite is deterministic. Prompts have non-zero failure rate for financial operations.

q: Agent resolves address change but forgets the return when customer asks for both. Architectural change?
opts: Decompose multi-concern requests into items, investigate in parallel with shared context, synthesize
Limit to one request per message
explain: Explicit decomposition with parallel investigation.

q: PostToolUse hook with mixed date formats from different tools. What should the hook do?
opts: Add format explanations to system prompt
Normalize all dates to consistent format (e.g., ISO 8601) before model processes them
Store dates in DB; agent queries with SQL
explain: PostToolUse hooks intercept and transform results before model processes them.

q: Policy: refunds over $500 need manager approval. Guaranteed compliance?
opts: Tool call interception hook blocking process_refund > $500, redirecting to escalation
explain: Tool call interception hooks deterministically enforce business rules.

q: Handoff to human agent transfers without context. What should handoff include?
opts: Structured summary: customer ID, root cause, refund amount, recommended action, what was investigated
explain: Structured handoff summaries include actionable information.

q: Key distinction between hooks and prompt instructions for enforcement?
opts: Hooks deterministic, prompts probabilistic; choose hooks when guaranteed compliance needed
Prompts more reliable due to context understanding
explain: Hooks operate programmatically outside model decision-making.

q: Resuming Claude Code session after code changes. What to do?
opts: Resume and trust model to detect changes
Start completely new session, redo everything
Resume and inform agent about specific file changes for targeted re-analysis
explain: Inform agent about specific file changes; preserves context while updating stale information.

q: Tools: analyze_content ('Analyzes content for insights') and analyze_document ('Analyzes documents for insights'). Agent often calls wrong one. First step?
opts: Rename tools and update descriptions to differentiate purpose, inputs, outputs, when to use
explain: Tool descriptions are primary tool selection mechanism. Renaming/updating is high-impact.

q: MCP tool returns generic {status:'error', message:'Operation failed'} for all failures. What to change?
opts: Add retry logic with exponential backoff
Eliminate errors and return successes with warnings
explain: Structured error metadata enables intelligent recovery.

q: Agent has 18 tools across customer/order/billing/shipping. Tool selection accuracy degraded. Primary cause and fix?
opts: Restrict to 4-5 tools per agent role
explain: Too many tools degrades selection. Scoped tool access per role.

q: Synthesis agent given web search tools 'for convenience' frequently searches instead of synthesizing. What to do?
opts: Remove web search; route complex verification through coordinator
explain: Agents tend to misuse out-of-specialization tools. Remove cross-specialization tools.

q: Always call extract_metadata before enrichment tools. Configuration?
opts: Define metadata first in tools array
explain: Forced tool selection guarantees specific tool execution. Switch to 'auto' for subsequent turns.

q: Share MCP server configurations across team. Where?
opts: Project's .mcp.json with env var expansion for credentials
explain: Project-scoped .mcp.json committed to VCS, env vars for personal credentials.

q: Personal experimental MCP server for testing. Where without affecting teammates?
explain: User-scoped ~/.claude.json applies only to that user, private.

q: MCP server has search_issues tool but agent uses built-in Grep. Likely cause?
opts: MCP tool description lacks clarity about capabilities
explain: Vague MCP descriptions cause agents to default to familiar built-ins.

q: Find all *.test.tsx files. Which built-in?
explain: Glob is dedicated for file path pattern matching.

q: Trace processRefund usage and dependencies. Recommended approach?
opts: Grep callers, then Read to follow imports incrementally
Glob all JS files, Read each
explain: Build understanding incrementally: Grep for entry points, then Read to trace.

q: New team member doesn't get coding standards; others do. Standards in CLAUDE.md. Most likely misconfiguration?
opts: Standards in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (user-level) on another dev's machine - not shared via VCS
explain: User-level config applies only to that user, not shared via VCS.

q: 500+ line CLAUDE.md hard to maintain. Best refactoring?
opts: Split into .claude/rules/ topic-specific files (testing.md, api-conventions.md, etc.)
Summary CLAUDE.md linking to external docs
explain: .claude/rules/ for topic-specific files. YAML frontmatter for path-scoping.

q: Monorepo with 5 packages, each different team. Best for per-package standards?
opts: @import in each package's CLAUDE.md to selectively include relevant standards
explain: @import lets each package reference its specific standards. Shared standards in common file.

q: Personal /deploy command for testing without affecting team. Where?
explain: User-scoped ~/.claude/commands/ for personal.

q: Skill performs verbose codebase analysis. Don't want output to pollute main context. Frontmatter?
explain: context: fork runs skill in isolated subagent context.

q: Skill should not execute shell or delete files. How to restrict?
opts: permissions.deny rules in .claude/settings.json (e.g., Bash(rm:*) plus Bash); allowed-tools frontmatter only PRE-APPROVES, it does NOT restrict tool access
explain: Per Claude Code docs, allowed-tools in skill frontmatter only PRE-APPROVES listed tools (skips permission prompts) — it does NOT restrict which tools are available. To block a skill from using specific tools, add deny rules in permissions settings (settings.json).

q: Code review skill should prompt for PR number when invoked without args. Frontmatter?
explain: argument-hint prompts for required parameters.

q: Need always-loaded coding standards AND on-demand workflows (review, deploy, migration). How organized?
opts: CLAUDE.md for always-loaded standards; skills (.claude/skills/) for on-demand workflows
explain: Separation keeps always-loaded context lean.

q: Verify which memory files loaded and diagnose inconsistent behavior. Command?
explain: /memory shows currently loaded memory files.

q: Personal variant of team skill with different behavior. Correct approach?
opts: Create personal variant in ~/.claude/skills/ with different name
Override team skill with same name in ~/.claude/skills/
explain: Personal customization with different name avoids conflicts.

q: Test files spread throughout (Button.test.tsx next to Button.tsx). Most maintainable for testing conventions?
opts: Rule files in .claude/rules/ with YAML frontmatter glob patterns like paths: ['**/*.test.tsx']
explain: Path-specific rules with globs apply by file type regardless of location.

q: Add null check to single function in one file. Bug clear from stack trace. Mode?
explain: Direct execution suits simple, well-scoped changes.

q: Restructure monolith into microservices, dozens of files, multiple valid approaches. Mode?
opts: Plan mode to explore, understand dependencies, design before changes
Direct, switch to plan only if stuck
explain: Plan mode for large-scale architectural decisions.

q: Multi-phase task uses Explore subagent for verbose discovery. Why preferred over main conversation?
opts: Isolates verbose output, returns summaries, preserves main context
explain: Explore prevents context exhaustion in multi-phase work.

q: CI command 'claude "Review this PR"' hangs. Fix?
explain: -p (print) flag for non-interactive.

q: Output structured findings for inline PR comments. Flags?
explain: --output-format json with --json-schema for machine-parseable output.

q: Generated tests duplicate existing suite. Fix?
opts: Provide existing test files in context
explain: Existing tests in context prevents duplication at generation time.

q: Same Claude session generates code AND reviews. Misses bugs. Why?
opts: Model retains generation reasoning, less likely to question own decisions
explain: Self-review bias: model retains generation reasoning.

q: Re-runs after new commits produce duplicate findings. Fix?
opts: Include prior findings in context, instruct to report only new/unaddressed
Only review files in latest commit
explain: Including prior findings allows distinguishing addressed vs new.

q: Improve CI test generation quality. Best CLAUDE.md configuration?
opts: Document testing standards, valuable test criteria, fixtures, examples of good vs low-value
explain: Contextual guidance with examples produces relevant high-quality tests.

q: Natural language descriptions inconsistent for code transformations. Most effective technique?
opts: 2-3 concrete input/output examples on real code
explain: Examples are most effective when prose interpreted inconsistently.

q: 40% false positives in 'comment accuracy' category. Devs ignoring all reviews. Most effective immediate action?
opts: Temporarily disable comment accuracy category to restore trust while improving prompts separately
explain: Disabling problematic category restores trust while improving offline.

q: Comment review prompt 'check that comments are accurate' produces false positives on technically imprecise but not misleading. Refine?
opts: 'Flag comments only when claimed behavior contradicts actual code behavior; do not flag merely incomplete or imprecise terminology'
explain: Explicit criteria define flagging rules; vague modifiers don't help.

q: Inconsistent formatting despite detailed prose. Most effective?
opts: Include 2-4 few-shot examples demonstrating exact desired format
explain: Few-shot examples are most effective for format consistency.

q: Few-shot examples cover common scenarios but fail on ambiguous ones. Improve?
opts: 2-4 targeted examples for ambiguous scenarios with reasoning for choice
Remove few-shot, rely on tool descriptions
explain: Few-shot for ambiguous cases should include reasoning, teaching frameworks not just answers.

q: PDF invoices: model occasionally returns JSON syntax errors. Most reliable for schema-compliant?
explain: tool_use with JSON schemas is most reliable for guaranteed compliance.

q: Three document types each different schema. Documents arrive without type labels. Configuration?
opts: tool_choice: 'any'; model must call a tool but can choose
explain: 'any' guarantees tool call while letting model choose appropriate schema.

q: vendor_address required field. Model fabricates when missing. Fix?
opts: Make optional/nullable so model can return null
explain: Optional/nullable lets model return null for missing info.

q: 5 known categories, occasionally new ones. Enum design?
explain: 'other' + detail string for extensible categorization.

q: Key limitation of tool_use with JSON schemas?
opts: Eliminates syntax errors but not semantic (totals not summing, wrong field placement)
explain: Strict schemas guarantee syntax not semantics.

q: Validation fails 15%. Retry resends same prompt. Only 10% success. Improve?
opts: Append validation errors to retry prompt with original document and failed extraction
explain: Retry-with-error-feedback: include doc, failed extraction, specific errors.

q: 30% of failures are missing info in source, not extraction errors. Retries fail. What to do?
opts: Identify when retries ineffective (info absent) vs effective (format mismatches), skip retries for absent-info
explain: Recognize when retries can't recover information that simply doesn't exist.

q: Find which code patterns trigger false positives in code review. Add to structured output?
explain: detected_pattern enables systematic false-positive analysis.

q: 5% of cases line items don't sum to stated_total but both extracted accurately. Handle?
opts: Extract calculated_total + stated_total + conflict_detected boolean; flag for review
explain: Document may contain inconsistency. Preserve accurate extraction; alert reviewers.

q: Pre-merge review (blocking) AND weekly audit reports. Switch both to Batch API for 50% savings?
opts: Switch weekly audit only; pre-merge needs synchronous (Batch up to 24-hour latency)
explain: Batch latency unsuitable for blocking; ideal for async.

q: 100 documents batched: 95 succeed, 5 fail (3 oversized, 2 transient errors). Handle?
opts: Resubmit only 5 (by custom_id), chunk oversized, retry transient unchanged
explain: Targeted resubmission with appropriate fixes per failure type.

q: Before batch-processing 10,000 documents, what first?
opts: Refine prompts on sample to maximize first-pass success
explain: Sample testing avoids 500 costly resubmissions on a 5% error rate.

q: PR with 14 files: detailed feedback for some, superficial for others, contradictory findings. Root cause?
opts: Attention dilution: inconsistent depth across files when processed together
explain: Attention dilution from too much content at once.

q: Same session generates and reviews code; 'review critically' or extended thinking don't help. Architectural change?
opts: Second independent Claude instance without generator's reasoning context
explain: Same session retains reasoning; independent instance is fresh.

q: Multi-turn agent loses exact refund amount during context summarization (says $47, actual $147.23). Preserve numerical?
opts: Extract transactional facts (amounts, dates, IDs, statuses) into persistent 'case facts' block included each prompt
Store in DB query each turn
explain: Progressive summarization risks condensing values. Persist case facts outside summarized history.

q: Three open issues: damaged return, billing dispute, address change. After resolving first, agent loses other two. Fix?
opts: Extract structured issue data (IDs, amounts, statuses) into separate context layer for multi-issue
Limit to one issue per conversation
explain: Multi-issue sessions need structured per-issue context layer.

q: lookup_order returns 40+ fields. Most requests need only 5. What to do?
opts: Trim verbose tool outputs to relevant fields
explain: Verbose outputs consume tokens disproportionate to relevance.

q: Findings from 5 subagents into single synthesis prompt. Middle (3rd, 4th) consistently underrepresented. Cause and fix?
opts: Lost-in-the-middle effect; place key findings summaries at beginning, use explicit section headers
explain: Known LLM behavior. Mitigate with summary upfront and headers.

q: Customer says 'I want to speak to a human' on simple order status. What should agent do?
opts: Answer first to show capability, then offer transfer
Honor explicit request immediately, transfer to human
Provide status and mention human available
explain: Explicit human requests are honored immediately.

q: Customer 'This is so frustrating, nothing works!' Issue is straightforward return. Agent escalates on sentiment. Correct?
opts: No, sentiment unreliable; acknowledge frustration while offering resolution; escalate only if reiterated
explain: Sentiment doesn't correlate with case complexity.

q: Multiple matches for 'John Smith'. Agent picks most recent account; misidentifications occur. What instead?
opts: Ask customer for additional identifiers (email, phone, order #)
explain: Request identifiers from customer for disambiguation.

q: Web search subagent error: {status:'error', message:'search unavailable'}. Coordinator has no info. Better?
opts: Structured error: failure type (timeout), attempted query, partial results, alternatives
explain: Structured error context enables intelligent recovery.

q: Subagent transient timeout, local retry succeeds. Report to coordinator?
opts: No, local recovery for transient; only propagate unresolvable
explain: Local recovery for transient failures; propagate only unresolvable.

q: Subagent successfully queries DB, finds zero matches. Returns {status:'error'}. Correct?
opts: No, valid empty result; indicate success with no matches
explain: Distinguish access failures from valid empty results.

q: After 2 hours exploring, agent gives inconsistent answers and references 'typical patterns' instead of specific classes. What's happening?
opts: Context degradation; reference accuracy deteriorates as context fills
explain: Verbose tool outputs accumulate, degrading specific reference accuracy.

q: Long exploration session. Maintain reliable access to key findings. Technique?
opts: Scratchpad files recording findings; reference for subsequent questions
explain: Scratchpad persists discoveries outside conversation context.

q: Investigate multiple aspects: tests, refund flow, APIs, schemas. Best approach?
opts: Subagents for specific questions while main coordinates high-level
explain: Subagent delegation isolates verbose exploration.

q: Multi-agent crashes during research. Restart loses all state. Pattern to prevent?
opts: Structured agent state exports (manifests); coordinator loads on resume
explain: Manifest exports enable checkpoint resume.

q: Context filling with verbose discovery from dozens of files. Command to reduce?
explain: /compact reduces by summarizing verbose discovery.

q: 97% aggregate accuracy, but users report errors on handwritten receipts specifically. What went wrong?
opts: Aggregate masks per-document-type performance; validate by type and field segment
explain: Aggregate metrics obscure type-specific gaps.

q: Automated high-confidence extractions (no human review). Monitor for degradation?
opts: Stratified random sampling for ongoing error rate measurement and pattern detection
explain: Stratified sampling catches degradation and validates confidence calibration.

q: Two credible sources: A says 'market grew 15%', B says '22%'. Synthesis?
opts: Preserve both with attribution and conflict annotation
explain: Preserve conflicts with attribution; let readers evaluate.

q: Subagents return prose. Synthesis loses source attribution. Fix?
opts: Require structured claim-source mappings (URLs, names, excerpts) preserved through synthesis
explain: Structured mappings ensure attribution survives synthesis.

q: Subagent A: 2023 data. Subagent B: 2025 data. Synthesis flags as 'contradictory'. Metadata to prevent?
opts: Publication or data collection dates in structured outputs
explain: Dates enable temporal interpretation, distinguishing trends from contradictions.
